items.headline: Council considers nine new homes plus accessory units on Old Trace Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking approval to subdivide a 1-acre vacant lot into nine single-family homes and seven junior accessory dwelling units on Old Trace Road. The project would add significant residential density to what is currently zoned as Residential Estate.

items.headline: Supermicro invests $2.5M in equipment at Rock Avenue facility
items.summary: The major tech hardware manufacturer is upgrading its San José location with a significant $2.5 million equipment installation. This represents continued investment in the company's local operations and manufacturing capacity.

items.headline: Summerhill redevelopment at El Camino Real moves forward
items.summary: Tree removal and replacement permits issued for the Palo Alto Greens parcel associated with Summerhill's major redevelopment project at 4345 El Camino Real. This signals progress on a significant mixed-use development that residents have been tracking.

items.headline: Mountain View approves bonds for new public safety building
items.summary: The City Council approved lease revenue bonds to finance a new public safety building project. This represents a significant infrastructure investment that will affect city services and taxpayer finances.

items.headline: City council reviews biodiversity and urban forest plan
items.summary: The council held a study session on a draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan, which will guide environmental and green space policies. This plan could affect tree preservation, park development, and environmental standards across Mountain View.

items.headline: Sunnyvale plans mixed-use development with 67 homes and retail
items.summary: The Planning Commission reviewed a proposed 4.22-acre project combining 67 residential units (55 townhomes and 12 flats) with 18,535 square feet of retail space, including the existing Speedy's Tacos location. The project would add significant housing and commercial activity to Sunnyvale while preserving an established local restaurant.

items.headline: Campbell revises business license tax rates
items.summary: The city council considered an ordinance updating business license tax rates in the municipal code, effective in the upcoming fiscal year. The outcome of this vote will affect what local businesses pay in licensing fees to operate in Campbell.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: City approves temporary giant signs near Levi's Stadium
items.summary: Santa Clara authorized large supergraphic signs on building surfaces around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. The signs will be temporary installations to promote the international soccer tournament being hosted at the venue.
